steps.title: Track the interrupted sentence
steps.content: The sentence begins an interruption with an em dash before in this case. That interruption identifies the earth as the porous rocks around Hot Springs. After that phrase, the sentence needs to return to the main idea: rainwater percolates downward to collect in a basin.

steps.title: Close the dash phrase
steps.content: Choice A never closes the interruption. Choice B uses a colon, and Choice D uses a comma, neither of which matches the opening dash. The em dash after Springs closes the interruption and lets to collect continue the sentence. The answer is C.
